summaries: The runner now stops on the first unexpected classification only after both post-command repository snapshots, transition checks, result record construction, and bounded safe diagnostic derivation complete.
The early-failure path cannot invoke later commands or the mutation-capable joint command, cannot persist a result artifact, and cannot advance the chain journal.
Focused and default command timeouts are now exact in both the committed JSON Schema and semantic catalog validator: 600,000ms for p0-1-focused and 360,000ms for every other entry.
Safe diagnostics remain limited to validated command ID, classification enum, and fixed reason categories; raw output, paths, environment values, and failure payloads are not emitted.
Catalog order, focused/non-P0.1 partition coverage, mutation-last placement, schema/catalog parity, signed review topology, and fail-closed artifact-chain behavior remain consistent.
No Critical or Important security or code-quality finding remains in the exact candidate.

verification: CodeGraph indexes were current: CC Gateway 118 files/4,744 nodes/18,565 edges and Sub2API 3,042 files/98,075 nodes/328,727 edges.
Recomputed frozen-base binary diff digests, plan digest, review-import digest, ancestry, exact heads, and clean worktrees; all matched the signed candidate contract.
Confirmed stale requirements/security attestations are absent from the candidate, so the next approval commit can add exactly the two fresh reviews as the sole delta.

Inspected commandRun ordering: mutation snapshots and record construction precede the immediate unexpected_classification failure; no later catalog iteration, result write, or chain transition occurs.

Reviewed the exact early-failure negative: audit contains only cc-build, CC/Sub ignored inventories and journal bytes remain identical, no GREEN artifact is written, and unsafe marker bytes are absent.
Independently validated the current catalog against both schema and semantic validator; mutated focused/default timeout values were rejected by both.
Independently verified fixed safe diagnostics for unsafe output, timeout, output overflow, infrastructure failure, and unexpected zero exit without raw marker leakage.
Root-recorded exact-candidate RED reproduced the pre-fix Sub inventory drift; the post-fix complete evidence suite and build both passed.
